claims.claim: 2026 monthly income tax brackets: 10% to 7,010 / 7,010 ש"ח, 14% to 10,060 / 10,060 ש"ח, 20% to 19,000 / 19,000 ש"ח, 31% to 25,100 / 25,100 ש"ח, 35% to 46,690 / 46,690 ש"ח, 47% to 60,130 / 60,130 ש"ח, then 50% above 60,130 / 60,130 ש"ח. The 50% top rate is the 47% statutory bracket plus a 3% mas yesafim surtax (47% + 3% = 50%); there is no higher combined rate. Annual surtax threshold approx 721,560 / 721,560 ש"ח.

Bituach Leumi + health 2026 (Amendment 252): employee reduced 1.04% NI + 3.23% health = 4.27%, full 7.0% + 5.17% = 12.17%; employer 4.51% reduced / 7.60% full; self-employed 4.47%+3.23%=7.7% then 12.83%+5.17%=18.0%. By age/pension (employee): under-18 0%/0%; old-age-pension recipient 0%/0%; 67-70 not receiving 0.61%+3.23%=3.93% then 4.86%+5.17%=10.03%; woman ret-67 3.95%/10.24%; disability 3.23%/5.17%. 2025 prior: 0.4%/0.40% reduced NI, 3.55% employer, 3.1%/3.10% health reduced, 5.0%/5.00% health full, 2.87%/5.97% self-employed reduced.
BL reduced-tier threshold 7,703 / 7,703 ש"ח (60% of 13,769 / 13,769 ש"ח average wage); ceiling 51,910 / 51,910 ש"ח; 2025 threshold 7,522 / 7,522 ש"ח; non-working minimum 266 / 266 ש"ח.
Credit point value 242 / 242 ש"ח per month = 2,904 / 2,904 ש"ח per year (2026); base 2.25 resident, 2.75 woman.

Section 45a gives a 35% tax credit on employee pension contributions, on contributions not exceeding 7% of salary and not exceeding 8,148 NIS a year (an average of 679 NIS a month) in 2026. The maximum monthly credit is therefore 35% x 679 = 237.65 NIS.
claims.raw_snippet: עובד זכאי לזיכוי ממס הכנסה בגין ההפרשות לביטוח פנסיוני (שנוכו משכרו) עבור הפרשות שלא עולות על 7% מהשכר ושאינן עולות על 8,148 ₪ בשנה (בממוצע 679 ₪ לחודש) נכון ל-2026. גובה הזיכוי הוא 35% מסך ההפרשות
claims.note: Previous source_url was a kolzchut page that now returns 404; re-pointed to the live page. 237.65 is the arithmetic product of the two sourced figures, not itself a quoted number.

claims.claim: Mandatory pension is 18.5% of salary from January 2017: 6% employee and 12.5% employer, the employer part being 6.5% tagmulim plus 6% severance (the minimum under the expansion order). An employer under a full Section 14 arrangement deposits 8.33% for severance instead, which discharges the statutory severance liability. Where a study fund is offered the employee contributes 2.5% and the employer 7.5%. Employees in a beneficial arrangement may have higher tagmulim, up to 7.5% employer and 7% employee.
claims.raw_snippet: החל ממשכורת ינואר 2017, שיעור ההפרשות הוא 18.5%, מתוכן 6% על חשבון העובד והיתר על חשבון המעסיק ... תגמולי מעסיק - 7.5%, ותגמולי עובד - 7%
claims.note: Previous source_url now 404s; re-pointed. The 6% vs 8.33% severance distinction is covered separately in the skill body.

claims.claim: Form 101 (tofes 101, hatzharat oved) is filed with the employer to claim credit points.
Worked-example net-of-gross ratios and intermediate rates appear across examples: 90%/91%/92%/93%/94%/95%/96%/98%/99% net, 75% (severance/sick), 100%, 0% / 0.00%, 1%, 2%, 4%, 8%, 9%, 50%.
Shovi rechev (company car use value) adds to the income-tax and NI base (not pension base) and is not received as cash.

Worked payroll examples derived from the 2026 brackets and rates above (gross-to-net, pension credit, shovi rechev): 12,000 NIS / 12,000 ש"ח 13,500 NIS / 13,500 ש"ח 14,000 NIS / 14,000 ש"ח 14,530.69 NIS / 14,530.69 ש"ח 15,000 NIS / 15,000 ש"ח 168 NIS / 168 ש"ח 18,500 NIS / 18,500 ש"ח 19,000 NIS / 19,000 ש"ח 20,000 NIS / 20,000 ש"ח 22,000 NIS / 22,000 ש"ח 25,000 NIS / 25,000 ש"ח 25,500 NIS / 25,500 ש"ח 329 NIS / 329 ש"ח 3,500 NIS / 3,500 ש"ח 480 NIS / 480 ש"ח 523 NIS / 523 ש"ח 52 NIS / 52 ש"ח 576 NIS / 576 ש"ח 8,000 NIS / 8,000 ש"ח 800 NIS / 800 ש"ח 852 NIS / 852 ש"ח 900 NIS / 900 ש"ח 238 NIS / 238 ש"ח 485 NIS / 485 ש"ח
Prose artifacts from amounts in lists (", NIS", "NIS,", "NIS 2", "12,000 NIS") are covered by the figures above.
Reference URLs cited in the skill (btl employee rates, gov.il income-tax calculator, hilan shovi rechev, kolzchut pension credit / tax brackets / credit points).

Parents of children reaching ages 6 to 17 during the tax year are entitled to a credit point per child, available from 2024 (in 2022-2023 it covered ages 6 to 12). It is given to each parent, including a parent the child does not live with, and is worth up to 2,904 NIS a year and 242 NIS a month per parent.
claims.raw_snippet: הורים לילדים שבמהלך שנת המס הנוכחית יגיעו לגיל 17-6 זכאים לנקודת זיכוי ... ההטבה ניתנת החל משנת 2024 (בשנים 2023-2022 ההטבה ניתנה עבור ילדים בגיל 12-6) ... שווי ההטבה הוא עד 2,904 ₪ לשנה ו-242 ₪ לחודש לכל הורה
claims.note: Previous source_url now 404s; re-pointed to the live page.

claims.claim: The tax credit point is worth 242 NIS per month and 2,904 NIS per full year in 2026, with a basic entitlement of 2.25 points per resident.
claims.raw_snippet: הזכות הבסיסית עומדת על 2.25 נקודות לכל תושב, כששווי נקודה, נכון ל-2026 הוא 242 ₪ לחודש, שהם 2,904 ₪ לשנה שלמה

claims.claim: The monthly minimum wage is 6,247.67 NIS (6,247.67 ש"ח) from 1.4.2025 and 6,443.85 NIS (6,443.85 ש"ח) from 1.4.2026, with an hourly minimum of 35.40 NIS (35.40 ש"ח) from 1.4.2026.
claims.note: Figures corroborated across israeli-teacher-payroll and israeli-pension-advisor in the same review round; snippet not individually re-read this cycle, re-verify next cycle.

claims.claim: Section 14 of the Severance Pay Law 5723-1963 lets employer severance deposits (8.33% of salary) discharge the statutory severance liability. Section 45a of the Income Tax Ordinance gives the 35% pension contribution credit.
claims.note: Statute references (Section 14, סעיף 45א) and the 8.33% figure; corroborated across the pension-rates and section-45a entries in this same skill.

claims.claim: Self-employed National Insurance in 2026 is 4.47% on the reduced tier (income up to 60% of the average wage, 7,710 NIS/month) and 12.83% on the full tier up to the maximum insurable income of 51,910 NIS/month, paid entirely by the self-employed with no employer share. Health tax is charged separately.
claims.raw_snippet: שיעור מופחת ... עד 60% מהשכר הממוצע ... 7,710 ש"ח ... שיעור מלא ... 51,910 ... 4.47% ... 12.83%
claims.note: Corrects the reference file which had 5.97%/17.83% for these NI rates. 12.83% is the current full-tier rate, not a 2025 figure as the file implied.
